citation: Quarrie, K., Gianotti, S., & Murphy, I. (2020). Injury Risk in New Zealand Rugby Union: A Nationwide Study of Injury Insurance Claims from 2005 to 2017. Sports Medicine, 50(2), 415-428.
injury_type_raw: dental injuries (subset of all injury claims)
extraction_notes: 13-year aggregate across all ages 5-40. Dental = 2.0% of 635,657 total injury claims = 12,713 dental claims (computed). Per-age claim rates (probability of at least one claim per season) are reported in age-stratified rows below; this row is the topline.

citation: Quarrie, K., Gianotti, S., & Murphy, I. (2020). Injury Risk in New Zealand Rugby Union: A Nationwide Study of Injury Insurance Claims from 2005 to 2017. Sports Medicine, 50(2), 415-428.
injury_type_raw: all rugby injury claims (any body site, dental is 2.0% overall)
rate_denominator_raw: probability (%) of at least one injury claim per season (any body site, not dental-specific)
extraction_notes: Age band 5-6 years. This row's rate is the probability of making at least one rugby injury claim per season (any body site), not the dental-specific rate. Dental fraction (2.0% topline) is likely lower in <13 yo (mostly soft-tissue) and higher in adolescents/adults; abstract doesn't stratify dental by age. Demographics: ~10% of players are female; female claim rate is 0.57x male overall.
